feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Batch Determination Not Triggering Automatically for Subcontracting Outbound Delivery in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to implement automatic batch determination for Subcontracting Outbound Deliveries created through the Subcontracting Cockpit app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

We have already completed the following configurations:

  • Maintained the required Batch Classes
  • Maintained Batch Search Strategy Records
  • Configured the Condition Tables
  • Maintained the Sort Sequence
  • Assigned the necessary batch determination settings

However, batch determination is still not being triggered automatically in the subcontracting outbound delivery. The system is not proposing any batch even though batch stock is available and the configuration appears to be in place.

Could you please advise on:

  • Any additional configuration required specifically for subcontracting outbound deliveries?
  • Whether batch determination is supported for deliveries created through the Subcontracting Cockpit app in Public Cloud?
  • Any prerequisites or known limitations that need to be considered?

For reference, we have also reviewed the batch determination log, which indicates that no valid search strategy is being found and the available batches do not correspond to the selection criteria.

Your guidance on resolving this issue would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks & Regards,
Kiran

entry.title: Error with automatic flexible Workflow for purchase requisition
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

 

We have an issue with the purchase requisition DIGITS in the productive environment, the flexible Workflow doesn’t appear correct for this document, and it is stuck in error during the approval process.

As you can see in the APP 'Flexible workflow Administrator' shows the error “Error while executing application callback (Method 'result_callback')”

As you can see, automatic flexible WF is active for purchase requisition (for both options: overall release of purchase requisition & Release of purchase requisition Item).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: WRICEF form - SAP S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
We are currently working on Supplier return Adobe form in S/4HANA Public Cloud, and we require Street, Postal code, Country field population in the Adobe Form.Since these fields are not available in the standard data form template(MMIM_GR4PO_COL_SLIP) , we implemented custom logic in BADI MMIM_ITEM_PROVIDE_DATA using CDS view I_Supplier based on supplier.Observation

  • The BAdI executes multiple times during the process.
  • During execution, the Street, Postal code, and Country values are correctly fetched and visible in the code tracing app.
  • However, the fields are missing in the XML and Adobe Form output.

    Clarification Required

    • Is there any recommended BAdI or enhancement approach to ensure data persistence for form output?

the generated solution is not working.

 

PS We got a reccomendation to use this Badi instead MMIM_FORM_DATA_ENRICHMENT but it is not found in custom logic app SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

entry.title: Special Tax conditions from components should roll-up to parent item for a BOM product in Sales
entry.content:
In sales order for our agronomy process, we are using a BOM with ERLA item category, which is then derived to TAQ in the sales order. The standard behavior of this product is to have the pricing at the parent item level, and the components are mainly used to know the products of the blend we will be creating.

However, in this scenario, each component will have their own agronomy taxes, which must be rolled up to the parent item for legal compliance, and these taxes cannot be manually added either. We cannot just set a condition record for the parent item on these taxes, since the components will be different for every sales order.

Is there a way to pull these taxes to the parent item?

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Create Sales Order with ERLA product
  2. Add pricing to parent item
  3. Confirm taxes are added at component level
  4. Save Sales Order
  5. Create Outbound Delivery
  6. PGI OBD
  7. Create Billing document

entry.title: User with Display-Only PO Catalog Able to Create PO
entry.content:
We have identified an authorization issue in our Production system where user was able to create Purchase Order despite being assigned only the display business catalog SAP_MM_BC_PURCH_DOC_DSP_PC through business role BR_WAREHOUSE_CLERK.

Our analysis shows that the application is available only via the display catalog. However, the user also receives BSART (Purchase Document Type) authorization through other assigned business roles  which appears to have enabled Purchase Order creation.

From an authorization perspective, a display-only application should not allow Create actions simply because another business role grants the underlying authorization object. Combining a display-only application with write authorizations from unrelated business roles results in unintended business access.

Please investigate this behavior and confirm whether this is a standard SAP design or an authorization defect. If this is expected behavior, please advise the SAP-recommended standard approach to ensure that users assigned the display-only Purchase Order catalog cannot create Purchase Orders in such scenarios.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: May I ask which CDS corresponds to the image app
Open Item Management is not getting activated
entry.content:
Although there are no transactions or balances for the G/L account in Display Line Items in General Ledger or Display G/L Account Balances, I am still getting an error when trying to activate Open Item Management through Schedule Accounting Data Changes.

The system is asking me to reverse certain accounting entries before the change can be made, even though no transactions or balances are visible for the G/L account in any ledger.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM: How to Print Only One GR Label Regardless of Receipt Quantity
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud regarding GR label printing and would appreciate your guidance.

When posting a Goods Receipt for an Inbound Delivery, the business requires the system to automatically print one GR label per inbound delivery line item, regardless of any quantity maintained on the document, such as:

Order Quantity
Entry Quantity
Storage Quantity
Order Price Quantity

The key requirement is that the label determination must be automatic and always result in exactly one label per line item.

I am aware of the "Number of Labels" field during PO Goods Receipt processing; however, this does not meet the requirement because it relies on manual user input rather than system-driven determination.

So far, I have explored the following options without success:

Output Parameter Determination - Adjusted the number of print copies. This only controls how many copies of an already-generated label are printed and does not influence the number of labels generated.

Label Form Assignment in the Product Master - Tested alternative label forms. All available label configurations appear to remain quantity-driven and do not support a fixed one label per item approach.

Number of GR Slips Configuration - Tested different values. The result is still calculated based on the receipt quantity and is not a 1:1 determination per line item.

Has anyone encountered a similar requirement in Public Cloud?

Any guidance or experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

James.

entry.title: Best Practice for Opening Balance Migration when Using Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are currently implementing a greenfield SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition project for a customer and are evaluating whether to activate Advanced Valuation from the initial go-live.

During cutover, we will migrate the opening balance sheet accounts from the customer's legacy ERP. The legacy system performs period-end foreign currency valuation before go-live, so the migrated balances will already include the unrealised foreign exchange revaluation as at the cutover date.

Our question is how the migrated opening balances should be treated before the first Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation run after go-live.

Does anyone know the recommended SAP approach in this scenario?

Specifically:

  • Should the migrated opening balances remain unchanged, with Advanced Valuation treating them as the opening carrying amounts and calculating only subsequent valuation movements?
  • Or is it necessary to manually reverse or adjust the legacy foreign currency revaluation after the opening balances have been migrated into SAP before running the first Advanced Valuation?

The configuration documentation mentions migration activities when activating Advanced Valuation, but it is not clear whether these apply only to customers migrating from the classic valuation functionality within SAP, or also to greenfield implementations migrating opening balances from a non-SAP legacy system.

Has anyone implemented this scenario in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition project? I'd be particularly interested in hearing from anyone who has activated Advanced Valuation from day one and can share how the opening balance migration was handled.

Thank you in advance for any insights or references to SAP documentation.

Wei

entry.title: Clarification on SSCUI 103301 - Assign Party ID Types to Business Partner
entry.content:
Clarification on the standard SAP behavior for this SSCUI 103301 – Assign Party ID Types to Business Partner..

Specifically, we would like to know:

  1. Is it mandatory to maintain an entry in SSCUI 103301 for every customer business partner?
  2. If yes, is this required only for customers receiving invoices through Peppol, or also for customers receiving electronic invoices via Email?
  3. Is there any standard SAP functionality to derive the Party ID automatically from Business Partner master data (for example Business Partner Number, VAT Registration Number, or another standard field) without maintaining individual SSCUI entries?
  4. Does SAP provide any supported mass maintenance or automation option for maintaining these assignments?
  5. Is it a supported configuration to create a single SSCUI entry with a blank Business Partner so that the Party ID Type is applied to all customers?

entry.title: Manage Purchasing Info Records app
entry.content:
We initially loaded Purchasing Info Records using the Migration Cockpit. Now we need to add new purchasing information and update existing records.

However, the Manage Purchasing Info Records app does not seem to provide a way to export all detailed data for mass maintenance.

Is there an equivalent of Master Data Export (similar to what is available for Product Master Data) for Purchasing Info Records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

If not, what is the recommended approach for extracting existing Purchasing Info Records, updating them in bulk, and re-uploading them?

entry.title: Verify Journal Entires Navigation not supported
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are getting an error ( Navigation not supported) when the user navigates Verify Journal Entries 

attached is the screenshot

entry.title: Title: SD billing – how to post the customer receivable to an alternative reconciliation account? (n
entry.content:
Title: SD billing – how to post the customer receivable to an alternative reconciliation account? (no manual field like MIRO)

Environment: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, release [enter your release, e.g. 2508]

What I want to achieve:
Post the customer receivable line of an SD billing document to an alternative reconciliation account – i.e. a different account from the default reconciliation account stored in the customer master.

What I have configured so far:
1. Created the alternative reconciliation account with account group RECN, flagged as a reconciliation account for account type customer (D).
2. Registered it in the Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts activity (SSCUI 102631) as an allowed alternative for the default reconciliation account.
3. Assigned the (default) reconciliation account in the customer's company code (FI) data.
4. Created a sales order, then an outbound delivery with goods issue, and proceeded to Create Billing Documents.

What I observe:
In Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced (MIRO) I can pick an alternative reconciliation account in the G/L Account field on the Details tab. But in the SD billing document I can't find any equivalent field to choose the reconciliation account – it looks like manual selection isn't offered here.

My question:
For the receivables (AR) side in SD billing, is manual selection simply not supported, and is the intended way to have the alternative reconciliation account derived automatically via Reconciliation Account Determination (condition technique) / the account determination procedure – i.e. the "Flexible Billing Document Posting" approach?

If so, which configuration am I missing? Do I still need to set up the access sequence, condition types, condition tables and condition records for reconciliation account determination, plus assign the account determination procedure – on top of the Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts step I already did? Or is there another intended way to route the SD billing receivable to the alternative account?

Any pointers to the correct config activities (or a working example) would be much appreciated. Thanks!

Tags: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SD, Billing, FI-AR, Account Determination

entry.title: I want to interface market rates from Hungary Central Bank to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
entry.content: I am looking for the steps to interface market rates from Hungary Central Bank to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system. I want to know how to upload or connect the rates using the proper API or data transfer method as described in SAP documentation.

entry.title: The Confirm button in My Purchase Requisitions app (F1639A) unavailable for prdct type group service
entry.content:
Hello sap Community, 

The "Confirm" button in the "My Purchase Requisitions" app (F1639A) appears to be available only when the purchase requisition product type group is "Material". In this case, it redirects users to the "Confirm Goods Receipt" app.
For purchase requisitions with the product type group "Service", the button is not available, and users must instead open the "Manage Service Entry Sheet" app (F2027).

Could the "Confirm" link be enhanced to redirect users to the "Confirm Goods Receipt" app when the product type group is "Material", and to the "Manage Service Entry Sheet" app when the product type group is "Service"?

Thank you

entry.title: Need APP to lookup component quantity under exist product
entry.content:
Greeting, SAP Expert

Our customer are looking for an APP to lookup the required component quantity under multiple highest level products. We tried several different apps, such as Find BOM for Component, Material Where-Used List in BOMs, or Explode bills of material, etc. however, non of them fullfill the requirement.

For example: 

                                 Product A1                                                                Product A2

         1 Component B1             1 Component B2                 1 Component B3           3 Component B1

1Comp C1     1Comp C2          1Comp C3        1Comp C1      1Comp C3    1Comp C2        1Comp C1     1Comp C2

Input C1
system can output:
Product A1 requires totally 2 C1, 
Product A2 requires 3 C1

Therefore, I'd like to ask SAP experts, is there any app in SAP public cloud able to acomplish this task?

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud | Unable to access the Worker Factsheet app
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and I am trying to access the Worker Factsheet app but I cannot find it.

  • App Name: Worker Factsheet
  • App ID: F7073_TRAN

This app belongs to the following business catalog:

  • Business Catalog: Worker - Display
  • Business Catalog ID: SAP_WFD_BC_WRK_DSP_PC

My user has already been assigned the following business role:

  • Business Role: Administrator - HR Info
  • Business Role ID: ZBR_ADMIN_HRINFO

However, I am still unable to access the Worker Factsheet app. Could anyone help me understand what I might be missing?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

QuocDT5

entry.title: Purchasing info record not updated after creation of PO
entry.content:
I created purchase order environment using a purchasing info record and changed the price in the purchase order. I selected the Info Update indicator.

Afterwards, I executed the job Determine and Update the Price of Purchasing Info Record. The job does not show any results.

I can see that the purchase order number has been added as the reference document in the purchasing info record. However, when I create a new purchase order using the same purchasing info record, the old price is still proposed.

Could you please confirm whether this is the expected system behavior? If not, could you advise which prerequisites or configuration settings are required for the updated price to be used in subsequent purchase orders?

entry.title: Mass change of Manage flexible workflow settings
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We currently utilize a three-level approval matrix for supplier invoice approval, with flexible workflows configured at the supplier level.

In the first level of this process, a specific individual was responsible for verifying and approving all supplier invoices and was included in every workflow. This individual has resigned, and we now need to replace them with a new approver.

Given that we currently have over 900 workflow items in the system, is there an option in the public cloud to perform a mass change of the approver within the manage flexible workflow settings?

 

Thanks

Naveen

entry.title: IFRS - Migration of Contracts, Link to asset master, valuation continuation in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts!

I need to migrate lease contracts and associated assets for my client. From the existing documentation, I understand that these must be migrated individually and then linked again in Valuation Management. We have successfully completed the migration; the asset master records and asset values match in both systems.

However, the situation is a bit more complicated when it comes to the contracts.

Our goal is to link the contracts to the corresponding migrated assets and to continue the valuation with the correct values starting from the migration date. I suspect that we need to define the correct combination of the following parameters:

  1. Start of Consideration

- What date should be entered here? Start of contract?

 

  1. First Posting From

- Is this the first day after the migration date? The date from which postings begin in the new system?

- This field also exists in the contract master record. Do the two always have to match?

 

  1. Probable End

- Mandatory or optional?

 

  1. Valuation Behavior

- The options here are Relative Difference (2), New Start (3), Absolute Difference (4), and Reduction (5).

Only with New Start (3) I have the option to select an asset. However, if I want to reuse an existing migrated asset, I must select one of the other behaviors. These, in turn, do not allow me to select an asset. What should be done here?

 

  1. Asset

- As mentioned in point 4, I can only select the corresponding migrated asset when the valuation behavior is “New Start” (3). However, the help section states that a new asset will be used in that case. This appears to be a contradiction.

 

We’ve already tried and simulated a number of things ourselves, but so far none of the approaches has resulted in a clean continuation of the valuation. Is there a documented, standardized procedure anywhere for our requirement to continue the contracts with migrated assets after migration?

I’d appreciate any advice!

Best regards,

Rado
